{{ define "main" }}

Declarative by default

Build Pixi games from scenes, state, and layout.

Pixora is a TypeScript-first framework for composing reactive game UIs with pixora(), pixora.scene(), and layout-driven nodes before reaching for low-level escape hatches.

Start with pixora() Play Space Invaders

Live reference example

The docs now ship a runnable game.

Space Invaders is bundled into the docs artifact, so GitHub Pages publishes the framework guide and a production build of the game together.

  • Declarative scenes with pixora.scene()
  • Reactive HUD and score updates
  • Layout-driven menus and overlays
  • Standalone output ready for GitHub Pages
Open standalone build Read the walkthrough

Why teams start with the declarative runtime

Declarative scenes

Compose screens from pure render functions instead of wiring scene classes first.

Reactive state

Drive game state with signals, computed values, and effects that stay close to the scene.

Layout-aware UI

Use flex and percent layout primitives to build HUDs, menus, overlays, and stage chrome.

Pixi-powered rendering

Keep direct access to Pixi.js performance while authoring against a higher-level runtime.

Imperative escape hatches

Interop with lower-level scene and application APIs when you need migration or fine-grained control.

Deployable examples

Use the same build pipeline for docs, examples, and a GitHub Pages development environment.

Start here

  • Install Pixora and Pixi.js
  • Build your first declarative scene
  • Understand the runtime returned by pixora()
  • Browse working examples and demos
